Skip to content

You said:

The application must be developed in three versions (v1, v2, v3). Each version must be: Developed in VS Code Pushed to GitHub (separate commits/branches) Built using Jenkins pipeline Containerized using Docker (tagged v1, v2, v3) Pushed to DockerHub Deployed in Kubernetes as 3 separate deployments/services Accessible via localhost (different ports) this how to create this project

ChatGPT said:

Nice — this is a full DevOps pipeline project (great for resume 🔥). I’ll break it into simple step-by-step phases so you can actually build it without getting stuck.

🚀 PROJECT OVERVIEW

You will build:

  • 1 App → 3 Versions (v1, v2, v3)

  • Use:

    • VS Code 💻

    • GitHub 🌐

    • Jenkins ⚙️

    • Docker 🐳

    • Kubernetes ☸️

🧩 STEP 1: CREATE BASIC APP (v1)

Create a simple Python app (Streamlit or Flask)

👉 Example: app.py

Python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/")
def home():
    return "Version 1 - Hello DevOps!"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=5000)

📦 STEP 2: DOCKERIZE (v1)

Create Dockerfile

dockerfile
FROM python:3.10

WORKDIR /app

COPY . .

RUN pip install flask

CMD ["python", "app.py"]

🐙 STEP 3: PUSH TO GITHUB

Bash
git init
git add .
git commit -m "v1 initial"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git
git push -u origin main

🔄 STEP 4: CREATE VERSIONS (v2, v3)

👉 Modify app:

v2

Python
return "Version 2 - Improved UI 🚀"

v3

Python
return "Version 3 - Production Ready 🔥"

👉 Use branches:

Bash
git checkout -b v2
git commit -am "v2 update"
git push origin v2

git checkout -b v3
git commit -am "v3 update"
git push origin v3

⚙️ STEP 5: JENKINS PIPELINE

Install Jenkins → create pipeline

👉 Jenkinsfile

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Clone') {
            steps {
                git 'https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git'
            }
        }

        stage('Build Docker Image') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t yourdockerhub/devops-app:${BUILD_NUMBER} .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push to DockerHub') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push yourdockerhub/devops-app:${BUILD_NUMBER}'
            }
        }
    }
}

🐳 STEP 6: PUSH TO DOCKER HUB

Login:

Bash
docker login

Tag images:

Bash
docker tag devops-app yourdockerhub/devops-app:v1
docker push yourdockerhub/devops-app:v1

docker tag devops-app yourdockerhub/devops-app:v2
docker push yourdockerhub/devops-app:v2

docker tag devops-app yourdockerhub/devops-app:v3
docker push yourdockerhub/devops-app:v3

☸️ STEP 7: KUBERNETES DEPLOYMENT

Create 3 deployment files

🔹 v1-deployment.yaml

YAML
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-v1
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-v1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: app-v1
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: yourdockerhub/devops-app:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000

🔹 Service (v1)

YAML
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: service-v1
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: app-v1
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30001

👉 Repeat for:

VersionPort
v130001
v230002
v330003

▶️ STEP 8: APPLY IN KUBERNETES

Bash
kubectl apply -f v1-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f v1-service.yaml

kubectl apply -f v2-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f v2-service.yaml

kubectl apply -f v3-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f v3-service.yaml

🌐 STEP 9: ACCESS APPLICATION

Open browser:

http://localhost:30001 → v1
http://localhost:30002 → v2
http://localhost:30003 → v3

🎯 FINAL ARCHITECTURE

VS Code → GitHub → Jenkins → Docker → DockerHub → Kubernetes → Browser

💡 BONUS (FOR HIGH MARKS)

Add:

  • UI (Streamlit instead of Flask)

  • CI/CD auto trigger from GitHub

  • Helm charts

  • Load balancer

🔥 IF YOU WANT

I can give you:

  • ✅ Full ready-made project folder

  • ✅ Jenkins setup (step-by-step screenshots style)

  • ✅ Kubernetes YAML for all versions

  • ✅ Advanced UI version (Streamlit)

Just say: “give full project code”


You said:

how to create this

ChatGPT said:

Got it — you’re asking specifically how to create the Dockerfile (Step 2). I’ll walk you exactly step-by-step like you’re doing it in VS Code.

🐳 STEP 2: CREATE DOCKERFILE (VERY SIMPLE)

✅ 1. Open your project in VS Code

Your folder should look like:

devops-app/
 ├── app.py

✅ 2. Create a new file

👉 In VS Code:

  • Right click on folder → New File

  • Name it:

Dockerfile

⚠️ IMPORTANT:

  • No .txt

  • No extension

  • Exactly: Dockerfile

✅ 3. Paste this code inside

dockerfile
FROM python:3.10

WORKDIR /app

COPY . .

RUN pip install flask

CMD ["python", "app.py"]

✅ 4. Save the file

Press:

Ctrl + S

✅ 5. Final folder structure

devops-app/
 ├── app.py
 ├── Dockerfile

▶️ STEP 3: BUILD DOCKER IMAGE

Open terminal in VS Code and run:

Bash
docker build -t devops-app:v1 .

▶️ STEP 4: RUN CONTAINER

Bash
docker run -p 5000:5000 devops-app:v1

🌐 Now open browser:

http://localhost:5000

You should see:

Version 1 - Hello DevOps!

⚠️ COMMON ERRORS (VERY IMPORTANT)

❌ Error: docker not recognized

👉 Install Docker Desktop

❌ Error: file not found

👉 Check:

  • File name is Dockerfile

  • Not dockerfile.txt

❌ Port not opening

👉 Make sure:

Python
app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=5000)

💡 SIMPLE UNDERSTANDING

LineMeaning
FROM python:3.10Base image
WORKDIR /appFolder inside container
COPY . .Copy your files
RUN pip install flaskInstall dependency
CMDRun app

🚀 NEXT STEP

After this, you’ll:
👉 Push image → DockerHub
👉 Create v2, v3
👉 Deploy in Kubernetes

If you want, I can next show you:

👉 How to install Docker + fix errors (Windows step-by-step)
👉 How to push to DockerHub (important)
👉 Full v1→v3 automation

Just tell 👍


You said:

the problem here is computer is not accepting this file as normal file its adding like .txt file

ChatGPT said:

Ahh yes — this is a very common Windows issue 👍
Your system is automatically saving Dockerfile as Dockerfile.txt.

Let’s fix it properly 👇

✅ 🔧 METHOD 1: RENAME CORRECTLY (FAST FIX)

Step 1:

Go to your project folder

Step 2:

Right click the file → Rename

If it shows:

Dockerfile.txt

👉 Change it to:

Dockerfile

⚠️ If it still becomes .txt again:

That means file extensions are hidden

✅ 🔧 METHOD 2: SHOW FILE EXTENSIONS (IMPORTANT)

Step 1:

Open File Explorer

Step 2:

Click View (top menu)

Step 3:

Enable:

✔ File name extensions

Step 4:

Now rename again:

Dockerfile.txt → Dockerfile

✔ Confirm the warning → Click Yes

✅ 🔧 METHOD 3: CREATE FROM TERMINAL (BEST METHOD 💯)

Open VS Code terminal and run:

Bash
touch Dockerfile

👉 If touch doesn’t work (Windows PowerShell):

PowerShell
New-Item Dockerfile

✅ 🔧 METHOD 4: CREATE IN VS CODE (SAFE WAY)

In VS Code:

  1. Click New File

  2. Type:

Dockerfile

  1. Press Enter

👉 If it still becomes .txt, then:

  • Go to Settings

  • Search: files.associations

  • Or use Method 2 (recommended)

🔍 HOW TO CHECK IF IT'S CORRECT

Correct file:

Dockerfile

❌ Wrong:

Dockerfile.txt

💡 PRO TIP

Inside VS Code, correct Dockerfile shows:

  • Docker icon 🐳

  • Syntax highlighting

🚀 AFTER FIX

Run:

Bash
docker build -t devops-app:v1 .

🔥 IF STILL NOT WORKING

Tell me:
👉 Screenshot or exact file name shown
👉 I’ll fix it instantly for you

Want next step?
👉 I can guide you DockerHub push + Kubernetes setup step-by-step


You said:

PS C:\Users\LOKESH> docker build -t devops-app:v1 . [+] Building 0.2s (1/1) FINISHED docker:desktop-linux => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile 0.0s => => transferring dockerfile: 2B 0.0s ERROR: failed to build: failed to solve: failed to read dockerfile: open Dockerfile: no such file or directory PS C:\Users\LOKESH>

ChatGPT said:

Good — this error actually tells us exactly what’s wrong 👇

failed to read dockerfile: open Dockerfile: no such file or directory

👉 Meaning: Docker cannot find your Dockerfile in the current folder

🔍 WHY THIS HAPPENED

You are running command in:

C:\Users\LOKESH

But your project (with Dockerfile) is not in this folder ❌

✅ FIX STEP-BY-STEP

🧭 STEP 1: GO TO YOUR PROJECT FOLDER

Find where your files are.

Example:

C:\Users\LOKESH\Desktop\devops-app

▶️ STEP 2: MOVE INTO THAT FOLDER

In PowerShell:

PowerShell
cd Desktop\devops-app

▶️ STEP 3: CHECK FILES

Run:

PowerShell
dir

You should see:

Dockerfile
app.py

⚠️ IF YOU DON'T SEE DOCKERFILE

👉 Then it's still wrong → fix name:

Dockerfile.txt ❌

Rename to:

Dockerfile ✅

▶️ STEP 4: BUILD AGAIN

PowerShell
docker build -t devops-app:v1 .

💡 QUICK CHECK (IMPORTANT)

Before running Docker:

PowerShell
dir Dockerfile

If output comes → ✅ correct
If error → ❌ file missing

🚨 COMMON MISTAKE YOU DID

You ran Docker build from:

C:\Users\LOKESH ❌

Instead of:

C:\Users\LOKESH\your_project_folder ✅

🔥 FINAL CORRECT FLOW

PowerShell
cd Desktop\devops-app
dir
docker build -t devops-app:v1 .

👍 IF STILL ERROR

Send me:
👉 Output of dir
👉 Your folder path

I’ll fix it in 1 message for you 💯

app.py

You said:

-a---l 04-04-2026 11.04 PM 190 app.py -a---l 04-04-2026 11.13 PM 97 dockerfile.txt PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile> rename dockerfile.txt Dockerfile rename : The term 'rename' is not recognized as the name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or operable program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again. At line:1 char:1 + rename dockerfile.txt Dockerfile + ~~~~~~ + CategoryInfo : ObjectNotFound: (rename:String) [], CommandNotFoundException + FullyQualifiedErrorId : CommandNotFoundException PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile>

ChatGPT said:

No worries 👍 — this is just a PowerShell command issue, not a big problem.

In PowerShell, rename sometimes doesn’t work directly. Use the correct command 👇

✅ ✔️ USE THIS COMMAND (WORKS 100%)

PowerShell
Rename-Item dockerfile.txt Dockerfile

✅ AFTER THAT → CHECK

PowerShell
dir

You should now see:

Dockerfile ✅
app.py

⚠️ IF STILL NOT WORKING (RARE CASE)

Try this:

PowerShell
Rename-Item "dockerfile.txt" "Dockerfile"

💡 ALTERNATIVE (MANUAL METHOD)

If command fails:

  1. Go to folder

  2. Right click → Rename

  3. Change:

dockerfile.txt → Dockerfile

  1. Click Yes on warning

🚀 NEXT STEP

Now run:

PowerShell
docker build -t devops-app:v1 .

🔥 IMPORTANT CHECK

If still error, run:

PowerShell
dir Dockerfile

If it shows file → ✅ ready
If not → ❌ still wrong name

👍 YOU'RE ALMOST DONE

This is the last small issue before everything works 💯

👉 After build works, say: next step
I’ll guide you to:

  • DockerHub push 🐳

  • Jenkins ⚙️

cmd

dir

app.py

cmd

cmd

app.py

cmd

cmd

You said:

explain this step now

ChatGPT said:

Got it 👍 — you want STEP 3: Push to GitHub explained clearly. Let’s do it simple and practical.

🐙 WHAT DOES “PUSH TO GITHUB” MEAN?

👉 You are uploading your project from your computer → to GitHub

So others (and Jenkins later) can access your code.

📁 YOUR CURRENT PROJECT

You already have:

agile/
 ├── app.py
 ├── Dockerfile

✅ STEP-BY-STEP: PUSH TO GITHUB

🔹 STEP 1: CREATE REPOSITORY

Go to 👉 GitHub

  1. Click New Repository

  2. Name:

devops-app

  1. Click Create

🔹 STEP 2: INITIALIZE GIT (IN YOUR FOLDER)

Open terminal inside:

C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile

Run:

Bash
git init

👉 This tells your folder: “you are now a Git project”

🔹 STEP 3: ADD FILES

Bash
git add .

👉 Means: “track all files”

🔹 STEP 4: COMMIT

Bash
git commit -m "v1 initial commit"

👉 This saves your version

🔹 STEP 5: CONNECT TO GITHUB

Copy your repo link from GitHub:

Example:

https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git

Run:

Bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git

🔹 STEP 6: PUSH CODE 🚀

Bash
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main

🎯 RESULT

Now your code is live on GitHub 🎉

You’ll see:

  • app.py

  • Dockerfile

🔥 WHY THIS STEP IS IMPORTANT

You need GitHub for:

ToolWhy
JenkinsPull code automatically
TeamShare code
Version controlTrack changes

⚠️ COMMON ERRORS

❌ git not recognized

👉 Install Git: https://git-scm.com

❌ authentication failed

👉 Use:

  • GitHub username

  • Personal Access Token (not password)

💡 SIMPLE FLOW

Your PC → Git → GitHub

🚀 NEXT STEP

After pushing, we’ll do:

👉 Create v2 & v3 versions (branches)
👉 Jenkins pipeline

Just say: next step 👍


You said:

tCache/Content.IE5/': Permission denied warning: could not open directory 'AppData/Local/Microsoft/Windows/INetCache/Low/Content.IE5/': Permission denied warning: could not open directory 'AppData/Local/Microsoft/Windows/Temporary Internet Files/': Permission denied

ChatGPT said:

Good — this error is very common 👍 and easy to fix.

🚨 WHY THIS ERROR HAPPENS

You ran:

Bash
git add .

👉 But you were in a folder that includes system folders like:

AppData/

Windows blocks access → so Git shows:

Permission denied

✅ IMPORTANT: THIS IS NOT A FAILURE

👉 These are just warnings, not errors
👉 Your project files can still work

BUT ⚠️ we should fix it properly (clean way)

✅ ✅ BEST FIX (DO THIS)

🔹 STEP 1: MAKE SURE YOU ARE IN CORRECT FOLDER

Run:

cmd
cd C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile

🔹 STEP 2: CHECK FILES

cmd
dir

You should ONLY see:

app.py
Dockerfile

🔹 STEP 3: ADD ONLY YOUR FILES (SAFE WAY)

Instead of git add ., use:

cmd
git add app.py Dockerfile

🔹 STEP 4: COMMIT

cmd
git commit -m "v1 initial commit"

🔹 STEP 5: PUSH

cmd
git push -u origin main

💡 EVEN BETTER (PRO LEVEL 🔥)

Create a .gitignore file

👉 Create file:

.gitignore

👉 Add this inside:

AppData/
*.log
__pycache__/

🎯 WHY THIS HAPPENS

IssueReason
Permission deniedWindows protected folders
git add .Adds EVERYTHING (bad practice sometimes)

🚀 SIMPLE RULE

👉 Always run Git inside your project folder only
👉 Avoid running from C:\Users\LOKESH

👍 YOU ARE DOING GREAT

You have already completed:

  • Docker build ✅

  • Folder setup ✅

Just small Git cleanup now.

🔥 NEXT STEP

After pushing successfully, say:

👉 next step

I’ll guide you:

  • v2 & v3 creation

  • Jenkins pipeline

  • Kubernetes deployment (main goal 🎯)


You said:

PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile> git push -u origin main error: src refspec main does not match any error: failed to push some refs to 'origin' PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile>

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 👍 this is a very common Git error — and easy to fix.

🚨 ERROR EXPLAINED

Bash
error: src refspec main does not match any

👉 Means:

  • You don’t have a commit yet OR

  • Branch main doesn’t exist yet

✅ FIX STEP-BY-STEP (DO EXACTLY)

🔹 STEP 1: CHECK STATUS

Bash
git status

👉 If you see:

No commits yet

→ That’s the problem ✅

🔹 STEP 2: ADD FILES

Bash
git add app.py Dockerfile

🔹 STEP 3: COMMIT (IMPORTANT 🔥)

Bash
git commit -m "v1 initial commit"

👉 Without this → push will NOT work

🔹 STEP 4: SET BRANCH NAME

Bash
git branch -M main

🔹 STEP 5: PUSH AGAIN

Bash
git push -u origin main

💡 WHY THIS HAPPENED

StepMissing
git addmaybe done
❌ git commitmissing
git pushfailed

👉 Git needs at least 1 commit before pushing

⚠️ IF STILL ERROR

Run and send me:

Bash
git status
git branch

I’ll fix instantly 👍

🎯 AFTER THIS WORKS

You will have:
✅ Code on GitHub
✅ Ready for Jenkins

🚀 NEXT STEP

After successful push, say:

👉 next step

We will build:

  • v2 & v3 versions (branches)

  • Docker tagging

  • Kubernetes deployment 🔥


You said:

Pasted text.txt
Document

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 👍 thanks — now I can clearly see the real problem from your file.

👉 This line is the key issue:

nothing added to commit but untracked files present

🚨 WHAT WENT WRONG

Even though you ran:

Bash
git add app.py Dockerfile

👉 Git is still trying to scan outside your folder (like ../../../AppData, etc.)

That means:

👉 Your Git repo was initialized in the wrong location earlier ❌

🔥 BEST FIX (CLEAN METHOD – DO THIS)

We will reset Git and do it properly ✅

✅ STEP 1: DELETE WRONG GIT CONFIG

Inside your agile folder run:

cmd
rmdir /s /q .git

👉 This removes the broken Git setup

✅ STEP 2: RE-INITIALIZE GIT (CORRECT WAY)

cmd
git init

✅ STEP 3: ADD ONLY YOUR FILES

cmd
git add app.py Dockerfile

✅ STEP 4: CHECK STATUS

cmd
git status

👉 Now it should show:

Changes to be committed:
  app.py
  Dockerfile

✅ STEP 5: COMMIT

cmd
git commit -m "v1 initial commit"

✅ STEP 6: ADD GITHUB REMOTE

cmd
git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git

✅ STEP 7: PUSH

cmd
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main

💡 WHY THIS HAPPENED

From your file:

../../../AppData/
../../../Downloads/

👉 Git was tracking your entire system 😅
That’s why:

  • Permission errors

  • Nothing committing

🎯 AFTER FIX

You’ll have:
✅ Clean repo
✅ Only your files tracked
✅ Push works

🚀 NEXT STEP

After push works, we move to:

👉 Create v2 & v3 versions (branches)
👉 Docker tagging
👉 Kubernetes deployment

👉 Just say: next step once GitHub push is successful 👍


You said:

/yourusername/devops-app.git PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile> git branch -M main >> git push -u origin main remote: Repository not found. fatal: repository 'https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git/' not found PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile>

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 👍 you’re very close now — this is the final small mistake.

🚨 ERROR EXPLAINED

Bash
remote: Repository not found.
fatal: repository 'https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git/' not found

👉 Problem:
You are still using:

Bash
yourusername ❌

That is just a placeholder, not your real GitHub username.

✅ FIX (VERY SIMPLE)

🔹 STEP 1: GO TO GitHub

  1. Open GitHub

  2. Click Your Profile (top right)

  3. Copy your actual username

Example:

Bash
lokesh123   ← (your real username)

🔹 STEP 2: CHECK YOUR REPO EXISTS

Make sure you created repo:

Bash
devops-app

👉 If not:

  • Click New Repository

  • Name: devops-app

  • Click Create

🔹 STEP 3: FIX REMOTE URL

Run this (replace with your username):

cmd
git remote remove origin
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/devops-app.git

Example:

cmd
git remote add origin https://github.com/lokesh123/devops-app.git

🔹 STEP 4: PUSH AGAIN 🚀

cmd
git push -u origin main

⚠️ IF ASKING LOGIN

👉 Use:

  • Username: your GitHub username

  • Password: ❌ (don’t use password)

Instead use:
👉 Personal Access Token (PAT)

🎯 RESULT

After success, you’ll see:

Bash
Enumerating objects...
Writing objects...
To https://github.com/yourusername/devops-app.git

And your files appear on GitHub 🎉

🔥 YOU’VE DONE SO FAR

✅ Docker build
✅ Git setup
✅ Almost GitHub push

🔹 HOW TO GET TOKEN (IMPORTANT 🔥)

On GitHub:

  1. Go to Settings

  2. Developer Settings

  3. Personal Access Tokens

  4. Generate Token (classic)

  5. Select:

    • ✅ repo

  6. Copy token

You’ll see something like:

To https://github.com/crazyloke1-hash/devops-app.git

cmd

cmd

  • main → v1

  • v2 → version 2

  • v3 → version 3

You said:

next step

ChatGPT said:

🔥 Great — now we move to the core DevOps part: Docker tagging + DockerHub push for v1, v2, v3

🐳 STEP: BUILD + TAG + PUSH (ALL VERSIONS)

✅ 🔹 STEP 1: LOGIN TO DOCKER HUB

cmd
docker login

👉 Enter:

  • Username: your DockerHub username

  • Password / Token

✅ 🔹 STEP 2: BUILD IMAGE FOR EACH VERSION

👉 Make sure you're in project folder:

cmd
cd C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile

🟢 Build v1 (main branch)

cmd
git checkout main
docker build -t devops-app:v1 .

🟡 Build v2

cmd
git checkout v2
docker build -t devops-app:v2 .

🔴 Build v3

cmd
git checkout v3
docker build -t devops-app:v3 .

✅ 🔹 STEP 3: TAG IMAGES FOR DOCKER HUB

👉 Replace with your DockerHub username (IMPORTANT)

cmd
docker tag devops-app:v1 crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1
docker tag devops-app:v2 crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v2
docker tag devops-app:v3 crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v3

✅ 🔹 STEP 4: PUSH TO DOCKER HUB 🚀

cmd
docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1
docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v2
docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v3

🎯 RESULT

On DockerHub, you will see:

  • devops-app:v1

  • devops-app:v2

  • devops-app:v3

🔥 VERIFY (OPTIONAL)

Run:

cmd
docker images

You should see all 3 versions

🚀 NEXT STEP (VERY IMPORTANT)

Now comes the final stage:

☸️ KUBERNETES DEPLOYMENT

You will:

  • Deploy 3 versions

  • Run on 3 different ports

  • Access via localhost

👉 Say: next step Kubernetes

I’ll give:

  • Full YAML files

  • Exact commands

  • No confusion 💯


You said:

explain this

ChatGPT said:

Got it 👍 — now I’ll clearly explain STEP 5: Jenkins Pipeline (this is the automation brain 🧠 of your project).

⚙️ WHAT IS JENKINS?

👉 Jenkins is a tool that:

  • Automatically pulls your code from GitHub

  • Builds Docker image 🐳

  • Pushes to DockerHub

  • (Later) deploys to Kubernetes

👉 In simple words:

GitHub → Jenkins → Docker → Kubernetes

🎯 WHY YOU NEED JENKINS

Without Jenkins ❌
👉 You manually run everything

With Jenkins ✅
👉 Everything runs automatically (CI/CD)

🧩 STEP-BY-STEP SETUP

✅ 🔹 STEP 1: INSTALL JENKINS

👉 Download from:

Jenkins official site

After install:

Open browser:

http://localhost:8080

Unlock Jenkins:

  • Go to this file:

C:\Program Files\Jenkins\secrets\initialAdminPassword

  • Copy password → paste in browser

✅ 🔹 STEP 2: INSTALL PLUGINS

Select:

Install Suggested Plugins

✅ 🔹 STEP 3: CREATE PIPELINE PROJECT

  1. Click New Item

  2. Name:

devops-pipeline

  1. Select:

Pipeline

✅ 🔹 STEP 4: CONNECT GITHUB

Scroll → Pipeline section

Choose:

Pipeline script from SCM

Set:

  • SCM → Git

  • Repo URL:

https://github.com/crazyloke1-hash/devops-app.git

  • Branch:

main

✅ 🔹 STEP 5: CREATE Jenkinsfile

👉 In your project folder, create file:

Jenkinsfile

📄 Paste this code:

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Clone') {
            steps {
                git 'https://github.com/crazyloke1-hash/devops-app.git'
            }
        }

        stage('Build Docker Image') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push to DockerHub') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }
    }
}

✅ 🔹 STEP 6: PUSH Jenkinsfile TO GITHUB

cmd
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "added Jenkins pipeline"
git push

▶️ STEP 7: RUN PIPELINE

In Jenkins:

👉 Click:

Build Now

🎯 WHAT WILL HAPPEN

Jenkins will:

  1. Pull code from GitHub

  2. Build Docker image

  3. Push to DockerHub

🔥 IMPORTANT SETUP (DON’T MISS)

🐳 Docker must be installed

🔐 Add DockerHub credentials in Jenkins:

Go to:

Manage Jenkins → Credentials

Add:

  • Username

  • Password / Token

💡 SIMPLE UNDERSTANDING

StageWhat happens
CloneGet code
BuildCreate Docker image
PushUpload to DockerHub

🚀 FINAL STEP AFTER THIS

After Jenkins works:

👉 Deploy in Kubernetes (3 versions, 3 ports)

👍 YOU ARE BUILDING A REAL DEVOPS PROJECT

This is:

  • Resume-level 💯

  • Interview-level 🔥

👉 Say: next step Kubernetes
I’ll give full deployment setup (very important final step)


You said:

just based on step 1 to 4 give step 5

ChatGPT said:

Got it 👍 — you want STEP 5 (Jenkins) strictly based on what you already did (Step 1–4). No extra confusion — just the exact continuation.

⚙️ STEP 5: JENKINS PIPELINE (CLEAN VERSION)

Now your current flow is:

VS Code → GitHub → (NOW Jenkins) → DockerHub

✅ 🔹 STEP 1: OPEN JENKINS

Open:

http://localhost:8080

👉 This is Jenkins dashboard

✅ 🔹 STEP 2: CREATE NEW PROJECT

  1. Click New Item

  2. Name:

devops-pipeline

  1. Select:

Pipeline

  1. Click OK

✅ 🔹 STEP 3: CONNECT YOUR GITHUB

Scroll → Pipeline

Select:

Pipeline script from SCM

Set:

  • SCM → Git

  • Repo URL:

https://github.com/crazyloke1-hash/devops-app.git

  • Branch:

main

✅ 🔹 STEP 4: CREATE Jenkinsfile (IMPORTANT)

In your project folder (agile), create file:

Jenkinsfile

📄 Paste THIS (based on your setup):

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {

        stage('Clone') {
            steps {
                git 'https://github.com/crazyloke1-hash/devops-app.git'
            }
        }

        stage('Build v1') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v1') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }

        stage('Build v2') {
            steps {
                sh 'git checkout v2 && docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v2 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v2') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v2'
            }
        }

        stage('Build v3') {
            steps {
                sh 'git checkout v3 && docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v3 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v3') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v3'
            }
        }
    }
}

✅ 🔹 STEP 5: PUSH Jenkinsfile TO GITHUB

cmd
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "added Jenkins pipeline"
git push

✅ 🔹 STEP 6: RUN PIPELINE

In Jenkins:

👉 Click:

Build Now

🎯 WHAT WILL HAPPEN

Jenkins will:

  1. Clone your repo

  2. Build:

    • v1

    • v2

    • v3

  3. Push all to DockerHub

⚠️ IMPORTANT (DON’T MISS)

🔹 Docker must be running

🔹 Add DockerHub login in Jenkins OR run once locally:

cmd
docker login

💡 SIMPLE UNDERSTANDING

GitHub → Jenkins → Docker build → DockerHub

🚀 NEXT STEP

After Jenkins works:

👉 Kubernetes deployment (final step)

👉 Say: next step Kubernetes
I’ll give full YAML + ports + deployment 💯

🔹 Change:

*/master ❌

👉 to:

*/main ✅

🧠 WHY THIS MATTERS

  • GitHub default branch = main

  • Jenkins is trying to pull master

  • So pipeline will fail ❌

✅ NEXT STEPS

🔹 1. Update branch

Change to:

*/main

🔹 2. Click:

Save

🔹 3. Run pipeline

ChatGPT said:

Don’t worry, this is normal. We just need to find the exact error.

🚨 WHAT YOUR SCREEN SHOWS

  • ❌ Red cross → build failed

  • ⏱ Took only ~6 sec → failed very early

  • ❗ “No changes” → Jenkins didn’t run properly

👉 This usually means:

Jenkinsfile not found OR wrong pipeline config

✅ FIRST: CHECK ERROR LOG (VERY IMPORTANT)

👉 Click:

Console Output

📌 This will show the real error.

🔍 MOST COMMON REASONS (LIKELY YOUR CASE)

❌ 1. Jenkinsfile not found

👉 Make sure your repo contains:

Jenkinsfile

📁 Inside GitHub repo root (same level as app.py)

❌ 2. Wrong pipeline type

Make sure you selected:

Pipeline script from SCM ✅

NOT:

Pipeline script ❌

❌ 3. Wrong branch earlier

You already fixed:

*/main ✅

Good 👍

❌ 4. Jenkinsfile not pushed

👉 VERY IMPORTANT:

cmd

git commit -m "added Jenkinsfile"

You said:

PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile> git add Jenkinsfile fatal: pathspec 'Jenkinsfile' did not match any files PS C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile>

ChatGPT said:

Good 👍 this error tells us exactly the issue:

Bash
fatal: pathspec 'Jenkinsfile' did not match any files

👉 Meaning: Jenkinsfile does NOT exist in your folder yet ❌

✅ FIX (DO THIS EXACTLY)

🔹 STEP 1: CREATE Jenkinsfile (CORRECT WAY)

Since Windows keeps adding .txt, use terminal method 👇

👉 In CMD run:

cmd
type nul > Jenkinsfile

🔹 STEP 2: VERIFY FILE

cmd
dir

You must see:

Jenkinsfile ✅
app.py
Dockerfile

🔹 STEP 3: OPEN IN VS CODE

  • Open Jenkinsfile

  • Paste this:

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Build v1') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v1') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }
    }
}

🔹 STEP 4: SAVE FILE

Press:

Ctrl + S

🔹 STEP 5: ADD + COMMIT + PUSH

cmd
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "added Jenkinsfile"
git push

⚠️ IMPORTANT CHECK

Run:

cmd
dir

👉 Make sure it is:

Jenkinsfile ❌ NOT Jenkinsfile.txt

🔥 WHY THIS HAPPENED

Windows was doing:

Jenkinsfile.txt ❌

Git cannot find:

Jenkinsfile ✅

🚀 AFTER THIS

Go back to Jenkins:

👉 Click:

Build Now

🎯 EXPECTED RESULT

No more:

Bash
type nul > Jenkinsfile ❌ (CMD only)

✅ FIX (POWERHELL METHOD — WORKS 100%)

🔹 STEP 1: CREATE Jenkinsfile

Run this:

PowerShell
New-Item Jenkinsfile

PowerShell
dir

You must see:

Jenkinsfile ✅

    stages {
        stage('Build v1') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v1') {

                sh 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }
    }
}

🚨 MAIN PROBLEM (VERY CLEAR)

👉 You still haven’t created the Jenkinsfile

That’s why:

Bash
fatal: pathspec 'Jenkinsfile' did not match any files

❌ ALSO YOU DID THIS WRONG

You typed:

PowerShell
return "Version 2..."

👉 That does NOT change your app.py file
👉 It only prints in terminal 😅

So:

Bash
nothing to commit ❌

✅ NOW FIX EVERYTHING (FINAL CLEAN STEPS)

🟢 STEP 1: CREATE Jenkinsfile (CORRECT WAY)

In PowerShell run:

PowerShell
New-Item -Name Jenkinsfile -ItemType File

🟢 STEP 2: CHECK FILE

PowerShell
dir

You must see:

Jenkinsfile ✅
app.py
Dockerfile

🟢 STEP 3: EDIT Jenkinsfile

Open in VS Code → paste:

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }
    }
}

👉 Save it

🟢 STEP 4: PUSH Jenkinsfile

PowerShell
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "added Jenkinsfile"
git push

🟢 STEP 5: FIX YOUR V2 & V3 (IMPORTANT 🔥)

You didn’t actually edit the file.

👉 Open app.py manually and change:

For v2:

Python
return "Version 2 - Improved Version 🚀"

Then:

PowerShell
git add app.py
git commit -m "v2 update"
git push origin v2

For v3:

Python
return "Version 3 - Final Production 🔥"

Then:

PowerShell
git add app.py
git commit -m "v3 update"
git push origin v3

Go to Jenkins → click:

Build Now

You said:

git push --set-upstream origin v3 To have this happen automatically for branches without a tracking upstream, see 'push.autoSetupRemote' in 'git help config'. C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile>

ChatGPT said:

Nice 👍 this message is NOT an error — it’s just a suggestion from Git.

✅ WHAT THIS MESSAGE MEANS

Bash
git push --set-upstream origin v3

👉 Git is saying:

“This branch (v3) is not linked to GitHub yet.
Use this command to connect it.”

🎯 SIMPLE EXPLANATION

SituationMeaning
main branchalready linked ✅
v2 / v3not linked yet ⚠️

✅ WHAT YOU SHOULD DO

Just run:

cmd
git push --set-upstream origin v3

👉 This will:

  • Push v3 to GitHub ✅

  • Link branch for future pushes ✅

💡 AFTER THIS

Next time you can just use:

cmd
git push

🔥 IMPORTANT (CHECK YOUR PROGRESS)

You should now have on GitHub:

  • main ✅

  • v2 ✅

  • v3 ✅

  • Jenkinsfile ✅

🚀 NEXT STEP (VERY IMPORTANT)

Now go back to Jenkins:

👉 Click:

Build Now

🎯 EXPECTED RESULT

Now Jenkins will:

  • Find Jenkinsfile ✅

  • Run pipeline ✅

  • Build Docker image ✅

👍 YOU ARE VERY CLOSE

After Jenkins works → final step:

👉 Kubernetes deployment ☸️🔥

👉 If Jenkins fails, send:
Console Output

I’ll fix it instantly 💯


You said:


ChatGPT said:

Good 👍 we’re almost there — Jenkins is running but still failing ❌
Now we just need to see WHY it failed.

🚨 WHAT YOUR SCREEN SHOWS

  • ❌ Build #1 and #2 failed

  • Jenkinsfile is now detected (good ✅)

  • But pipeline is failing during execution

✅ NEXT STEP (VERY IMPORTANT)

👉 Click this:

Last build (#2)

Then click:

Console Output

🎯 WHY THIS IS IMPORTANT

👉 The real error is inside Console Output

Right now we are guessing ❌
We need exact error to fix 💯

🔍 MOST LIKELY ERROR (BASED ON YOUR SETUP)

One of these:

❌ 1. sh command not working (VERY COMMON)

Your Jenkinsfile has:

groovy
sh 'docker build ...'

👉 This works only on Linux

⚠️ You are on Windows → it will fail

✅ FIX (VERY IMPORTANT 🔥)

Change Jenkinsfile:

❌ OLD:

groovy
sh 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'

✅ NEW:

groovy
bat 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'

Full FIXED Jenkinsfile:

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }
    }
}

✅ AFTER FIX

Run:

cmd
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "fixed windows command"
git push

🚀 THEN

Go to Jenkins → click:

Build Now

🎯 EXPECTED RESULT

✅ Build stage works
✅ Push stage works
❌ No more failure

🔥 YOU ARE SUPER CLOSE

This is a Windows vs Linux issue — very common

🚀 NEXT STEP

After this works:

👉 Add v2 & v3 to pipeline
👉 Kubernetes deployment ☸️🔥

👉 If still fails, send:
Console Output screenshot

I’ll debug instantly 💯

ChatGPT said:

Ahh 👍 I see the confusion — you tried to run:

cmd
bat 'docker build ...'

👉 That is WRONG place ❌

🚨 IMPORTANT UNDERSTANDING

CommandWhere to use
batONLY inside Jenkinsfile ⚙️
docker buildCMD/PowerShell 💻

❌ WHAT YOU DID

You ran in CMD:

cmd
bat 'docker build ...'

👉 That will NEVER work ❌

✅ WHAT YOU SHOULD DO

🔹 STEP 1: FIX Jenkinsfile (NOT CMD)

Open your Jenkinsfile and change:

❌ OLD:

groovy
sh 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'

✅ NEW:

groovy
bat 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'

🔹 STEP 2: SAVE FILE

Press:

Ctrl + S

🔹 STEP 3: PUSH TO GITHUB

cmd
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "fixed windows bat command"
git push

🔹 STEP 4: RUN JENKINS

Go to Jenkins → click:

Build Now

🎯 WHAT WILL HAPPEN

Jenkins will now:

  • Use bat (Windows command) ✅

  • Build Docker image ✅

  • Push to DockerHub ✅

💡 SIMPLE LOGIC

Your PC (Windows) → use bat  
Linux server → use sh

This is the final fix for Jenkins

ERROR: Unable to find Jenkinsfile

👉 This means:
Your Jenkinsfile is STILL not present in the GitHub main branch

🔍 ROOT CAUSE (IMPORTANT)

You created Jenkinsfile locally ✅
But Jenkins is reading from:

main branch ❗

👉 And your Jenkinsfile is probably:

  • not pushed ❌

  • or in another branch (v2 / v3) ❌

cmd

cmd
git push origin main

Replace everything inside with this:

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }
    }
}

Ctrl + S

    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Check Docker') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker --version'
            }
        }

        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push crazyloke1-hash/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }
    }
}

ChatGPT said:

🔥 Perfect — now we have the real error. You’re VERY close.

🚨 ROOT PROBLEM (CLEAR)

From your log:

ERROR: failed to connect to the docker API
//./pipe/docker_engine

👉 Meaning:

Docker is installed ✅
BUT Docker service is NOT running ❌

🎯 SIMPLE EXPLANATION

CheckStatus
docker --version✅ works
Docker engine running❌ NOT running
Jenkins access❌ fails

✅ FIX (DO THIS EXACTLY)

🔹 STEP 1: START DOCKER DESKTOP

👉 Open:

Docker Desktop

Wait until you see:

Docker is running ✅

🔹 STEP 2: VERIFY IN TERMINAL

Run:

cmd
docker ps

👉 If it shows container list → ✅ correct
👉 If error → Docker still not started ❌

🔹 STEP 3: RESTART JENKINS (IMPORTANT 🔥)

After Docker starts:

cmd
net stop jenkins
net start jenkins

👉 This reconnects Jenkins to Docker

🔹 STEP 4: RUN JENKINS AGAIN

Go to Jenkins → click:

Build Now

🎯 EXPECTED RESULT

Now:

✅ Docker build works
✅ Push stage runs
❌ No more docker error

🔥 WHY THIS HAPPENED

Jenkins tried:

connect to docker_engine ❌

But Docker service was OFF

🚀 YOU ARE NOW 99% DONE

After this works:

👉 Add v2 & v3 in pipeline
👉 Kubernetes deployment ☸️🔥

Problem → Docker not running
Fix → Start Docker Desktop + restart Jenkins

C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile>docker ps CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile>

ChatGPT said:

🔥 Perfect — this output means:

Bash
docker ps ✅

👉 Docker is RUNNING properly now 🎉

🚨 WHY JENKINS STILL FAILED BEFORE

Even though Docker is running now:

👉 Jenkins was started before Docker started

So Jenkins couldn’t connect to Docker ❌

✅ FINAL FIX (VERY IMPORTANT)

🔹 STEP 1: RESTART JENKINS

Run this in CMD:

cmd
net stop jenkins
net start jenkins

🔹 STEP 2: WAIT 10–15 SECONDS

Let Jenkins fully restart

🔹 STEP 3: RUN PIPELINE AGAIN

Go to Jenkins → click:

Build Now

✔ Build → success
✔ Push → success

denied: requested access to the resource is denied

DockerHub login required

cmd
docker login

You said:

give me the full code

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 👍 now I’ll give you the FINAL COMPLETE Jenkinsfile (v1 working) using your Docker username lokesh1926.

✅ FULL JENKINSFILE (COPY THIS)

Open Jenkinsfile and paste:

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {

        stage('Check Docker') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker --version'
            }
        }

        stage('Build v1') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t lokesh1926/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v1') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push lokesh1926/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }

    }
}

💾 SAVE + PUSH

Run:

cmd
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "final working Jenkinsfile v1"
git push

🚀 RUN IN JENKINS

Go to Jenkins → click:

Build Now

🎯 EXPECTED RESULT

✔ Check Docker → ✅
✔ Build v1 → ✅
✔ Push v1 → ✅ 🎉

🔥 AFTER SUCCESS (IMPORTANT NEXT)

Now we upgrade to full requirement:

👉 You need:

  • v1

  • v2

  • v3

🚀 ADVANCED VERSION (ALL 3 VERSIONS)

After v1 works, update Jenkinsfile to this:

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {

        stage('Build v1') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t lokesh1926/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v1') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push lokesh1926/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }

        stage('Build v2') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t lokesh1926/devops-app:v2 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v2') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push lokesh1926/devops-app:v2'
            }
        }

        stage('Build v3') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t lokesh1926/devops-app:v3 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v3') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push lokesh1926/devops-app:v3'
            }
        }

    }
}

🚀 FINAL STEP AFTER THIS

👉 Kubernetes Deployment ☸️
(3 versions → 3 ports)

💯 YOU ARE DOING PRO LEVEL DEVOPS

You’ve already done:

  • GitHub ✅

  • Jenkins ✅

  • Docker ✅

cmd

code Jenkinsfile

🔹 STEP 4: PASTE FINAL CODE

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {

        stage('Check Docker') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker --version'
            }
        }

        stage('Build v1') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t lokesh1926/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push v1') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push lokesh1926/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }

    }
}

Open Jenkinsfile again:

cmd
code Jenkinsfile

🔥 PASTE THIS FULL WORKING CODE

groovy
pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {

        stage('Check Docker') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker --version'
            }
        }

        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker build -t lokesh1926/devops-app:v1 .'
            }
        }

        stage('Push') {
            steps {
                bat 'docker push lokesh1926/devops-app:v1'
            }
        }

    }
}

cmd
git add Jenkinsfile
git commit -m "final working pipeline"
git push

You said:

step 7 give

ChatGPT said:

🔥 Great — you’ve reached the FINAL STEP (Step 7: Kubernetes Deployment) 🎉
Now we will deploy your app in Kubernetes with 3 versions (v1, v2, v3)

☸️ STEP 7: KUBERNETES DEPLOYMENT

✅ 🔹 STEP 1: START KUBERNETES

👉 If using Docker Desktop:

  • Open Docker Desktop

  • Go to Settings → Kubernetes

  • Enable:

Enable Kubernetes ✅

Wait until it shows:

Kubernetes is running ✅

✅ 🔹 STEP 2: VERIFY

Run:

cmd
kubectl version --client
kubectl get nodes

👉 You should see node info ✅

✅ 🔹 STEP 3: CREATE YAML FILES

In your project folder create:

k8s-deployment.yaml

📄 FULL YAML (COPY THIS)

YAML
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: devops-app-v1
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: devops-app-v1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: devops-app-v1
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: devops-app
        image: lokesh1926/devops-app:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: devops-service-v1
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: devops-app-v1
  ports:
    - port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30001
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: devops-app-v2
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: devops-app-v2
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: devops-app-v2
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: devops-app
        image: lokesh1926/devops-app:v2
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: devops-service-v2
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: devops-app-v2
  ports:
    - port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30002
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: devops-app-v3
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: devops-app-v3
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: devops-app-v3
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: devops-app
        image: lokesh1926/devops-app:v3
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: devops-service-v3
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: devops-app-v3
  ports:
    - port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30003

✅ 🔹 STEP 4: APPLY CONFIG

cmd
kubectl apply -f k8s-deployment.yaml

✅ 🔹 STEP 5: VERIFY

cmd
kubectl get pods
kubectl get services

🌐 STEP 6: ACCESS YOUR APPS

Open browser:

http://localhost:30001 → v1
http://localhost:30002 → v2
http://localhost:30003 → v3

🎯 FINAL RESULT

You now have:

VersionURL
v1localhost:30001
v2localhost:30002
v3localhost:30003

🔥 CONGRATULATIONS 🎉

You built a FULL DEVOPS PIPELINE:

✅ VS Code
✅ GitHub
✅ Jenkins
✅ Docker
✅ DockerHub
✅ Kubernetes ☸️

💯 THIS IS INTERVIEW LEVEL PROJECT

🚀 OPTIONAL NEXT

If you want upgrade:

  • CI/CD auto trigger

  • Helm charts

  • Ingress setup

👉 If anything fails, send error — I’ll fix instantly 💪

cmd
cd C:\Users\LOKESH\OneDrive\Desktop\agile

cmd
echo. > k8s.yaml
code k8s.yaml

✅ STEP 5: PASTE THIS CODE

YAML
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-v1
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-v1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: app-v1
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: lokesh1926/devops-app:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: service-v1
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: app-v1
  ports:
    - port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30001
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-v2
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-v2
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: app-v2
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: lokesh1926/devops-app:v2
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: service-v2
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: app-v2
  ports:
    - port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30002
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-v3
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-v3
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: app-v3
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: lokesh1926/devops-app:v3
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: service-v3
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: app-v3
  ports:
    - port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30003

👉 Save file

✅ STEP 6: APPLY KUBERNETES

cmd
kubectl apply -f k8s.yaml

✅ STEP 7: CHECK RUNNING

cmd
kubectl get pods
kubectl get svc

👉 All should be Running

🌐 STEP 8: OPEN IN BROWSER

http://localhost:30001 → v1
http://localhost:30002 → v2
http://localhost:30003 → v3

You now have:

✅ 3 Deployments
✅ 3 Services
✅ 3 Versions running

✅ STEP 4: PASTE THIS (IMPORTANT)

YAML
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-v1
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-v1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: app-v1
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: lokesh1926/devops-app:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: service-v1
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: app-v1
  ports:
    - port: 5000
      targetPort: 5000
      nodePort: 30001

👉 Save (Ctrl + S)

cmd
kubectl apply -f k8s.yaml
